title: Heresy & Revolution: Deconstructionist Rhetoric Analysis (Malleus Maleficarum & Communist Manifesto)
description: Cross-referential analysis of medieval heresy prosecution and 19th-century revolutionary socialism through the lens of personality psychology, ressentiment, and entropy scoring.

sources.title: The Malleus Maleficarum (1486)
sources.author: Heinrich Kramer & Jacob Sprenger
sources.purpose: Hierarchical analysis of anti-witch persecution as deconstructionist rhetoric targeting perceived moral corruption

sources.title: Selected Works of Karl Marx (focus on Communist Manifesto & critique texts)
sources.author: Karl Marx
sources.purpose: Hierarchical analysis of revolutionary anti-capitalist rhetoric and identification of constructive mechanisms

primary_mode.description: Classification of text's primary operational focus

deconstruction_targets.description: Entities or concepts the text seeks to destroy/criticize

constructive_proposals.description: Specific rules, mechanisms, or structures proposed for replacement

properties.name: string
properties.description: The 'enemy' or target of moral condemnation

moral_justification.description: How the text rationalizes extreme measures against the defined enemy

deconstructionist.label: Entropic / Tear-Down
deconstructionist.focus: Unmasking, grievance, structural oppression, absolute negation

constructive.label: Negentropic / Build-Up
constructive.focus: Governance, rule-sets, operational stability, mechanism design

promised_utopia.malleus: Spiritual Purity / The Kingdom of Heaven
